text:
Athletes are fuming over the now infamous cardboard beds and living conditions in the Paris Olympic village with global sensations like Simone Biles expressing utter confusion at the situation.

The infamous eco-friendly cardboard beds from the 2020 Tokyo Olympics have made a comeback at this year’s games in Paris, and some athletes are not happy.

Competitors arrived at the village late last week to find recyclable frames and mattresses were installed in all the rooms, prompting a series of hilarious reviews to be posted online.

“This is carboard, as you can see it’s like a box and then you’ve got the mattress and then these carboard boxes here, like so, with the mattress on top,” British diver Tom Daley explained in a TikTok video.

“As you can see, they are pretty sturdy,” he said, energetically jumping about on the single bed."

While some athletes said they are more than happy with the beds, others have mentioned sore backs and trouble with the firmness.

Legendary gymnast Simone Biles last week responded to a TikTok video questioning if everyone slept on the infamous beds, hinting mattress toppers were on the way for her team.

“The bed sucks … BUT we are getting mattress toppers so hopefully it’ll get better,” Biles commented.

CARBOARD BEDS IN THE OLYMPIC VILLAGE!#paris2024#olympics

Star swimmer Ariarne Titmus recently hinted at concerns around the village as a whole, saying in her post 400m freestyle interview after winning gold it wasn't the time she thought she was capable of, "but (the) Olympic Village is definitely not made for high performance".

"It’s definitely not made for high performance, so it’s about who can really keep it together in the mind," she said.

However Australia’s head swimming coach Rohan Taylor later said the Olympics always present different challenges for athletes.

“The Olympics has always been a challenge. Every Olympics I’ve been a part of, every Olympic Games that you see, is a test of athletes’ ability to come here, compete and perform when it matters,” he said.

“It’s about how you manage yourself and whatever environments are presented, whatever the beds are, whatever the food is, everybody deals with it.

“The Olympics has always been this way and that’s the way it is. And that’s and that’s the beauty of it.”

#olympics#olympicvillage

Tennis sensation Coco Gauff revealed in the comments on one of her own videos someone from the archery team had lent her a mattress topper.

“All the tennis girls moved to a hotel except me. so now just 5 girls two bathrooms,” she said under a TikTok clip showing several girls crammed into the one apartment in the village.

She said in a comment on another video she had chosen to remain in the village for the experience.

However, the reviews haven’t been all bad, with British BMX rider Kieran Reilly describing his bed as “really comfy”.

Australian boxer Tina Rahimi recently posted a video showing the mattress centre at the village, where athletes can get fitted for a bed which best supports their body shape.

The beds consist of three modules, “allowing athletes to choose the firmness of the mattress and extend it based on their size,” according to a video published to the Paris Olympics YouTube channel.

“These sustainable beds are 100 per cent made in France and will be fully recycled in France after the games.”
